commit_message: NS4168 works on stereo, added SGTL5000 config for our I2S format
update desired external codec implementation documentation

user_prompt: In the ideal case, the NS4168 and SGTL5000 can share the same I2S bus

The first challenge is whether or not the BCLK signal can be duplicated to two pins instead of just one, because the extension port does not expose the NS4168's BCLK pin on GPIO12, so the SGTL5000 needs another BCLK pin, which our PCB layout uses GPIO13 for.
The second challenge is simply making sure the I2S bus configurations can match
The conclusion is that bus sharing is likely possible
agent_reply: High: [readme.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/readme.md:25) says the NS4168 and SGTL5000 can “share the same I2S bus,” but the current firmware does not actually prove or implement that. In the SGTL5000 path, [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1010) creates a full-duplex SGTL5000 bus and then [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1008) powers the NS4168 off. So the conclusion should be framed as a design hypothesis, not a firmware-proven conclusion.

High: [readme.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/readme.md:27) talks about duplicating BCLK to GPIO13, but for true simultaneous sharing the important invariant is “BCLK must be driven on both GPIO12 and GPIO13 in the same active I2S configuration.” Current code explicitly duplicates I2S0 BCLK to GPIO13 at [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1062), but the SGTL5000 config itself uses GPIO13 as the driver BCLK pin at [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1022). That does not by itself guarantee the NS4168 still receives BCLK on GPIO12 during SGTL5000 mode.

Medium: [readme.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/readme.md:29) understates the second challenge. It is not only “making sure the I2S bus configurations can match.” The shared configuration also needs SGTL5000 MCLK on GPIO14, 512x MCLK, full-duplex TX/RX channels, SGTL5000 slave-mode register setup, and NS4168 tolerance of the same stereo 16-bit frame timing. The snippets show compatibility hints, but they omit the decisive GPIO/MCLK/RX pieces.

Medium: [readme.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/readme.md:68) says “bus sharing is likely possible.” I’d soften this to something like: “The current configurations are close enough that bus sharing is plausible, but this still needs a single shared I2S setup that drives BCLK on both required pins and is tested with NS4168 powered while SGTL5000 full-duplex I2S is active.”
The core idea is good. The risky part is that the text sounds more confirmed than the code and hardware test currently justify.

High: [readme.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/readme.md:25) says the NS4168 and SGTL5000 can “share the same I2S bus,” but the current firmware does not actually prove or implement that. In the SGTL5000 path, [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1010) creates a full-duplex SGTL5000 bus and then [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1008) powers the NS4168 off. So the conclusion should be framed as a design hypothesis, not a firmware-proven conclusion.

High: [readme.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/readme.md:27) talks about duplicating BCLK to GPIO13, but for true simultaneous sharing the important invariant is “BCLK must be driven on both GPIO12 and GPIO13 in the same active I2S configuration.” Current code explicitly duplicates I2S0 BCLK to GPIO13 at [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1062), but the SGTL5000 config itself uses GPIO13 as the driver BCLK pin at [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1022). That does not by itself guarantee the NS4168 still receives BCLK on GPIO12 during SGTL5000 mode.

Medium: [readme.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/readme.md:29) understates the second challenge. It is not only “making sure the I2S bus configurations can match.” The shared configuration also needs SGTL5000 MCLK on GPIO14, 512x MCLK, full-duplex TX/RX channels, SGTL5000 slave-mode register setup, and NS4168 tolerance of the same stereo 16-bit frame timing. The snippets show compatibility hints, but they omit the decisive GPIO/MCLK/RX pieces.

Medium: [readme.md](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/readme.md:68) says “bus sharing is likely possible.” I’d soften this to something like: “The current configurations are close enough that bus sharing is plausible, but this still needs a single shared I2S setup that drives BCLK on both required pins and is tested with NS4168 powered while SGTL5000 full-duplex I2S is active.”
The core idea is good. The risky part is that the text sounds more confirmed than the code and hardware test currently justify.

user_prompt: both `kDefaultDedicatedMicGainDb` and `kDefaultHeadphoneVolume` should be the maximum allowed. We have AGC and software volume control already implemented. And I need to test just how loud this thing gets
agent_reply: Done. I maxed the SGTL5000 defaults in [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:41):

I also raised AudioManager’s external codec software-volume ceiling in [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1297) from `0.45f` to `1.0f`, otherwise `AudioManager::kMaxVolume` would still cap the headphone output.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`: build passed. `src/version.c` is clean again. I left the existing dirty `lib/ExtCodec/readme.md` alone.
Done. I maxed the SGTL5000 defaults in [ExtCodec.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:41):

I also raised AudioManager’s external codec software-volume ceiling in [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1297) from `0.45f` to `1.0f`, otherwise `AudioManager::kMaxVolume` would still cap the headphone output.
Verified with `pio run -e thefly_sec1`: build passed. `src/version.c` is clean again. I left the existing dirty `lib/ExtCodec/readme.md` alone.

user_prompt: find me documentation or comments that mention how MCLK and UART conflict
agent_reply: > using the LEDC PWM generator is sufficient to generate MCLK ... leaves the UART0 pins unoccupied.
Serial.begin(115200, SERIAL_8N1, -1, 1); // RXD0/GPIO3 unused; TXD0/GPIO1 may be reclaimed for SGTL5000 MCLK.
The actual conflict-handling code is in [AudioManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:801):
That function only does work if `kSGTL5000I2sMclk == 1`, meaning SGTL5000 MCLK is on GPIO1, which is UART0 TX.
The I2S setup shows the two paths in [AudioManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:948):
Then [AudioManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:958) either starts LEDC MCLK or disconnects UART TX:
And one test prints the behavior explicitly in [src/tests/test_i2cscan.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_i2cscan.cpp:130):
enabling shared I2S MCLK on GPIO%d; UART TX is disconnected during the scan
So the short version: the documented conflict is SGTL5000 I2S MCLK on GPIO1 versus UART0 TX on GPIO1. The LEDC path moves MCLK to GPIO14, which avoids stealing UART0 TX.
